channel.title: Daivik's Journals
channel.description: Monthly deep-dives into supply chain, logistics, freight markets, and global economics by Daivik Suresh.

item.title: The Invisible Half — Inside the World of Indirect Procurement
item.description: Every company obsesses over what goes into the product. Far fewer watch the money spent keeping the lights on — and that's exactly where the savings hide.

item.title: The Analytics Awakening — How AI Is Rewriting Business Intelligence
item.description: The dashboard isn't going anywhere. But the way we talk to data just changed forever.

item.title: The Data Center Boom — AI Infrastructure & The Supply Chain Behind the Intelligence
item.description: Behind every AI model is a physical supply chain — silicon, copper, concrete, and an enormous amount of electricity.

item.title: Oil, OPEC+ & The Energy Transition — Crude Markets in a Fractured World
item.description: Oil doesn't just fuel trucks. It fuels the entire calculus of supply chain economics.

item.title: Wars, Weapons & Supply Chains — How Geopolitical Conflicts Reshape Global Logistics
item.description: Every missile fired, every shipping lane disrupted, every sanction imposed — the supply chain absorbs the cost.

item.title: 2026 Supply Chain Outlook — Agentic AI, Reshoring & The Road Ahead
item.description: The companies that built for resilience in 2024-2025 are now reaping the compound returns. The rest are still catching up.

item.title: EOY 2025 — Two Years Down, A Decade of Change Ahead
item.description: The year tested every assumption. Supply chains that survived are stronger for it.

item.title: The Holiday Surge — Peak Season Logistics & Last-Mile Innovation
item.description: The supply chain doesn't sleep in November. It runs hardest when everyone else is celebrating.

item.title: Circularity & Waste — Building Supply Chains That Give Back
item.description: This is a key topic for any business that wants to leave less of a carbon footprint and maximize efficiency.

item.title: Bonded Warehousing & Cybersecurity — Securing the Physical and Digital Supply Chain
item.description: September's foundational elements — securing supply chains from both physical and digital angles.

item.title: AI Is Everywhere — Warehousing, Trucking, Forecasting & Agentic Intelligence
item.description: August has been quite the month — and it's all about AI in supply chain.

item.title: Manufacturing Types, Supplier Relationships & Equities to Watch
item.description: July's heat is on — and we're diving into the manufacturing landscape shaping our supply chains.

item.title: Wartime Supply Chains & The One Big Beautiful Bill Act
item.description: June's sunny vibes couldn't hide the weight of what's happening in global trade.

item.title: Demand Planning & The Future of Data-Driven Decisions
item.description: April showers brought more than just May flowers — beneath the surface, significant developments were brewing.

item.title: The Chip Behind Everything
item.description: Semiconductors — The Commodity Powering the Modern World

item.title: Canals, Robots & the Flow of Everything
item.description: Trade Canals Under Pressure — Robotics Rising

item.title: The Inventory Imperative
item.description: Inventory Optimization — The Backbone of Supply Chain

item.title: The Tariff Era Begins
item.description: 60% on China, 25% on neighbors — supply chain teams worldwide are scrambling to model the implications.

item.title: EOY 2024 — A Year of Disruption, Innovation & Resilience
item.description: Twelve months. Twelve journals. Rate cuts, port strikes, an election, AI going mainstream — and a look ahead to 2025.

item.title: Post-Election Supply Chain — Trump Wins, Markets Surge
item.description: A historic market rally, sweeping tariff proposals, and a reshoring opportunity — the 2024 election resets trade policy for a generation.

item.title: The Port Strike — 14 Ports, 25,000 Workers, $1B/Day
item.description: The first East Coast port strike since 1977 exposes deep tensions between automation and labor — and the evolution of ports from 1.0 to 4.0.

item.title: Rate Cuts Land & Nuclear Energy Enters Supply Chain
item.description: The Fed's first cut since 2020 reshapes supply chain finance — and nuclear power enters the sustainability conversation.

item.title: 1 Year of Daiiv Journals — Quantum Computing Deep Dive
item.description: One year in. A deep look at quantum computing's transformative potential for supply chain management.

item.title: Maritime Cargo, GDP Growth & The Election Trade
item.description: Port of LA gets smarter, the Fed eyes September, and the election reshapes supply chain strategy.

item.title: Swarm Robotics, AI Forecasting & Quantum Computing in Logistics
item.description: Formal education will make you a living; self-education will make you a fortune." — Jim Rohn

item.title: Digital Twins, Spend Management & The Fed Holds Steady
item.description: Wealth is not about having a lot of money. It's about having a lot of options." — Daivik Suresh

item.title: SAP's AI Copilot, Self-Driving Semis & Rate Cut Reality Check
item.description: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest." — Benjamin Franklin

item.title: NVIDIA's Blackwell, Generative AI & Crypto ATHs
item.description: Money is Power! With enough of it, you can build anything, even a rocket to the moon." — Scrooge McDuck

item.title: Oil Surges, 3D Printing & The Self-Driving Revolution
item.description: Money can't buy happiness, but it will certainly get you a better class of memories." — Ronald Reagan

item.title: New Year, New Market — PMI Rebounds & AI Takes the Wheel
item.description: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step." — Lao Tzu

item.title: EOY 2023 — Reflecting on a Year of Resilience & Innovation
item.description: Passion is the currency of progress. Invest in your fire and watch the world blaze with possibility.

item.title: Holiday Season, Freight Surge & Watching the Watchlist
item.description: The best time to plant a tree was 20 years ago. The second best time is now.

item.title: Market Correction, Crude Oil & Supply Chain Innovations
item.description: Money is like gasoline. It gets you where you want to go, but it's not the destination.

item.title: Port Congestion, Yellow's Collapse & The Rise of AI
item.description: Money is a tool, not a goal.

item.title: Freight, Oil & The American Supply Chain
item.description: Good Morning, Good Evening, and Good Night — wherever you're reading this.
